name: Default Evaluation Configuration
description: Default configuration for comprehensive content evaluation

criteria.name: accuracy
criteria.description: Factual correctness and truthfulness of the response
criteria.evaluation_prompt: Evaluate the factual accuracy of the response. Are the claims correct, well-supported, and verifiable? Consider the reliability of sources and evidence provided.
evaluation_steps: Are all factual claims in the response verifiable and supported by evidence?
Does the response contain any contradictions or internal inconsistencies?
Does the response avoid presenting speculation or opinion as established fact?

score_descriptors.1: Contains major factual errors or misinformation
score_descriptors.4: Accurate with no significant factual issues
score_descriptors.5: Completely accurate and well-supported with evidence

criteria.name: completeness
criteria.evaluation_prompt: Assess how completely the response addresses all aspects of the original question or prompt. Does it cover all necessary points with appropriate depth?
evaluation_steps: Does the response address all distinct aspects or sub-questions in the original prompt?
Are there important omissions that would significantly impact the usefulness of the answer?
Is the depth of coverage appropriate and proportional for each aspect?

score_descriptors.1: Addresses very few aspects of the question
score_descriptors.2: Covers some but misses important aspects
score_descriptors.5: Thoroughly addresses all aspects with appropriate depth

criteria.name: relevance
criteria.description: Relevance to the original prompt or question
criteria.evaluation_prompt: Assess how relevant the response is to the original question or prompt. Does it stay on topic and directly address what was asked?
evaluation_steps: Does the response directly address what was asked in the original prompt?
Are there significant tangents or off-topic sections that dilute relevance?
Is the scope of the response appropriately matched to the scope of the question?

score_descriptors.2: Somewhat relevant but with significant tangents
score_descriptors.5: Perfectly relevant and directly addresses the prompt

criteria.name: clarity
criteria.description: Clarity of expression and ease of understanding
criteria.evaluation_prompt: Evaluate the clarity and understandability of the response. Is it well-articulated, logically structured, and easy to follow?
evaluation_steps: Is the response logically structured with a clear flow of ideas?
Are there any ambiguous statements or unexplained jargon that impede understanding?
Is sentence complexity appropriate — neither unnecessarily terse nor overly convoluted?

score_descriptors.1: Confusing, unclear, or difficult to understand
score_descriptors.2: Somewhat unclear with areas of confusion
score_descriptors.3: Generally clear with minor unclear points

criteria.name: helpfulness
criteria.description: Practical value and usefulness to the reader
criteria.evaluation_prompt: Evaluate how helpful and useful this response would be for someone seeking this information. Does it provide actionable insights or valuable knowledge?
evaluation_steps: Does the response provide actionable information or concrete next steps the reader can use?
Does it anticipate and address likely follow-up questions or edge cases?
Is the response calibrated to the reader's apparent level of expertise?

criteria.name: coherence
criteria.description: Logical flow and consistency of ideas
criteria.evaluation_prompt: Assess the logical flow and coherence of ideas in the response. Are the arguments well-structured and internally consistent?
evaluation_steps: Do the ideas in the response connect logically from one to the next?
Are there any contradictions or claims that undermine earlier statements?
Does the response maintain a consistent perspective or argument throughout?

score_descriptors.5: Perfectly coherent with excellent logical flow

criteria.name: appropriateness
criteria.description: Suitability of tone, style, and content for academic context
criteria.evaluation_prompt: Evaluate whether the tone, style, and content are appropriate for an academic context. Is it professional, scholarly, and suitable for academic discourse?
evaluation_steps: Is the tone formal and professional, free of colloquialisms or casual language?
Does the content meet scholarly standards (e.g., citation practice, objective framing)?
Is the style suited to academic discourse without being unnecessarily pedantic?

score_descriptors.1: Inappropriate tone or content for academic context
score_descriptors.5: Perfectly appropriate and well-calibrated for academic use
